title: lupinus albus var. termis
intro: Lupinus albus var. termis, also known as the white lupine, is a variety of the white lupin plant that is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its tall spikes of fragrant white flowers and its edible seeds. The medicinal properties of Lupinus albus var. termis include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to its high content of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve insulin sensitivity, making it a valuable herb for managing conditions such as diabetes and cardiovascular disease. Herbal preparations of Lupinus albus var. termis can be made into teas, infusions, and supplements, and are often used to support heart health and reduce the risk of chronic diseases.
